### SLA Time-to-Done Breach Rate

The proportion of PSOM tickets that missed the "Time to done" SLA (a 16-hour
resolution target), based on the latest weekly snapshot.

This metric reflects how well the team is resolving tickets within the agreed
timeframe. Only tickets where the SLA clock was active are included; tickets
with no SLA configuration are excluded.

*Alternative names*:

- time to done SLA breach rate
- resolution SLA compliance
- time-to-done SLA failure rate

**Calculation.** Filter to the most recent `date` partition and to tickets where
`sla_time_to_done_breached` is not empty (i.e., the SLA was active). Divide
the count of tickets where `sla_time_to_done_breached = 'True'` by the total
count of SLA-active tickets.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

```sql
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS total_sla_tickets,
    SUM(CASE WHEN sla_time_to_done_breached = 'True' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS breached_count,
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN sla_time_to_done_breached = 'True' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE)
        / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS breach_rate
FROM jira.psom
WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM jira.psom)
  AND sla_time_to_done_breached != '';
```

## R

```r
psom <- tbl(con, in_schema("jira", "psom"))
latest_date <- psom |> summarise(d = max(date, na.rm = TRUE)) |> pull(d)

psom |>
  filter(as.Date(date) == as.Date(latest_date), sla_time_to_done_breached != "") |>
  summarise(
    total_sla_tickets = n(),
    breached_count    = sum(as.integer(sla_time_to_done_breached == "True"), na.rm = TRUE),
    breach_rate       = sum(as.integer(sla_time_to_done_breached == "True"), na.rm = TRUE) /
                          as.double(n())
  )
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* The rate is a proportion between 0 and 1. Tickets with
no active SLA (empty `sla_time_to_done_breached`) are excluded from both
numerator and denominator. Check `total_sla_tickets` to understand the sample
size underlying the rate.

A ticket that is still open and within its 16-hour window is recorded as not
breached (`"False"`), the same as a ticket that met the SLA. The rate on a live
snapshot is therefore a lower bound: some of those open tickets may still breach
before they are resolved. It is most meaningful once the snapshot's tickets are
largely resolved.
